name: Ken Haas
headline: Enterprise technology leader building AI-enabled transformation with governance
summary: Executive technology leader with 19+ years across cloud, cybersecurity, data, AI, customer success, and enterprise transformation. Ken builds adoption systems where people, platforms, partners, and governance turn technology into measurable business outcomes.

stats.label: years across cloud, cyber, data, AI, and customer success
new cloud commitment after accelerating a prior term
partner-program revenue impact tied to Platinum Club recognition

lenses.label: Transformation
lenses.title: Turn enterprise intent into durable adoption
lenses.summary: Ken's core lane is customer success at enterprise scale: translating executive priorities into cloud, cybersecurity, data, AI, and modern-work adoption with operating cadence and measurable outcomes.
points: Completed a $250M cloud commitment five-year term in three years.
Grew monthly cloud consumption from $1.8M to $15.5M and helped secure a new $600M commitment.
Led top-tier financial-market customer success across Azure, Modern Work, Dynamics, security, data, and AI.

lenses.label: AI governance
lenses.title: Use AI where judgment, auditability, and safety stay visible
lenses.summary: The through-line from enterprise leadership to AT-0 is governed automation: AI systems that accelerate work without hiding approvals, privacy boundaries, runtime truth, or human accountability.
points: Built AT-0 from scratch as a governed AI operating lab.
Uses human-in-the-loop review for sensitive actions instead of silent mutation.
Frames privacy, approvals, kill switches, and observability as product requirements.

lenses.label: Architecture
lenses.title: Bridge business outcomes and technical platforms
lenses.summary: Ken has held leadership, business, and technical roles across hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, mainframe modernization, data fabric, customer success, and enterprise architecture.
points: Advanced more than $150M in software revenue as a senior enterprise cloud and security architect.
Built cloud and security go-to-market plays across Red Hat, LinuxONE, and hybrid cloud.
Holds CISSP, Open Group architecture credentials, AWS Solutions Architect Associate, and Azure Fundamentals.

lenses.label: Community
lenses.title: Lead with empathy, inclusion, and practical access
lenses.summary: Ken's walking-deck themes are consistent: earn trust with empathy, create clarity of vision, include more perspectives, and sponsor people into confidence and results.
points: Led and supported D&I, allyship, and neurodiversity activities.
Mentors underrepresented groups navigating corporate and startup environments.

highlights.label: Enterprise transformation
highlights.text: Leads customer success programs that connect executive priorities, partner motion, and technology adoption across regulated financial-services environments.

highlights.label: Governed AI builder
highlights.text: Built AT-0 from scratch to prove AI capability through real product boundaries: approvals, privacy, memory, domain workflows, and operator review.

highlights.label: Platform depth
highlights.text: Experienced across Azure, AWS, IBM, Google Cloud, hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, data, AI, containers, Kubernetes, and mainframe modernization.

highlights.label: Leadership principles
highlights.text: Walking-deck themes: earn trust with empathy, incubate innovation through inclusion, produce clarity of vision, and keep improving by one percent.

timeline.focus: Leads customer success for top-tier financial-market clients across Azure, Modern Work, Dynamics, cybersecurity, data, AI, partner adoption, and business value realization.

timeline.role: Customer Success, Cloud, Security, and Enterprise Architecture Leadership
timeline.focus: Held technical, sales, customer success, architecture, and leadership roles spanning hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, data, containers, Kubernetes, and financial-services transformation.
Built a governed AI operating lab from scratch to demonstrate practical AI capability, operator workflows, approval gates, memory, privacy boundaries, and domain-specific agents.
Supports underrepresented groups navigating business, corporate, and startup paths through mentorship, access, and practical community leadership.

education: MBA, University of North Carolina Kenan-Flagler Business School
BS, Business Administration and Computer Science, Furman University

at0.headline: AT-0 is the build proof: governed AI from blank page to working operating system.
at0.summary: AT-0 demonstrates Ken's AI capability by turning leadership principles into a real product system: human-in-the-loop agents, domain-specific workflows, memory review, privacy boundaries, runtime checks, and Cloudflare-hosted public surfaces.

capabilities.label: Operator control
capabilities.text: Helm-style UI patterns for Ask, review, inbox, memory, and operator handoff.

capabilities.label: Governed agents
capabilities.text: Approval gates, safety belts, kill switches, and no silent sensitive actions.

capabilities.label: Domain apps
capabilities.text: Family, financial, medical, talent, print, data-source, and Beacon-style workflows with explicit boundaries.

capabilities.label: Production habits
capabilities.text: Tests, PR gates, deployment checks, runtime truth, observability, and rollback paths.

writing.title: New era of computing in banking
